channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Thu, 09 Jul 2026 03:50:59 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: “Who on Charlie’s team betrayed”: Candace Owens raises explosive questions over Ben Shapiro security updates
item.description: Candace Owens has sparked fresh controversy after claiming Ben Shapiro’s security team received updates while Charlie Kirk was being taken to a hospital after the 2025 shooting. Owens says the communication raises major questions, while Jeremy Boreing and other critics argue it was routine security coordination. The dispute has deepened ongoing tensions between Owens, Daily Wire figures, and Turning Point USA associates.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Computer scientist who created world's first chatbot in the 1960s spent his whole life warning that AI should never replace humans, and the reason will shock you
item.description: In the 1960s, Joseph Weizenbaum pioneered the first chatbot, named Eliza, which unveiled a startling truth: humans could emotionally bond with machines. This insight led him to caution against the potential of AI usurping human empathy. Weizenbaum's assertions about the risks of deceiving machines fostering misplaced trust echo powerfully in today's landscape of artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: TOI Tech Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Michael Schumacher: 'You win a race, the next race it’s a question mark. Are you still the best or not?'
item.description: Michael Schumacher emphasizes a vital truth: past victories are no assurance of future triumphs. In every event, champions must earn their recognition anew. This notion transcends sports, resonating within business and scientific realms alike. Ongoing dedication and adaptability are crucial for maintaining excellence and significance. True mastery is an evolving journey rather than a static achievement.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Fyodor Dostoevsky: 'What is hell? I maintain that it is the suffering of being unable to love'
item.description: Fyodor Dostoevsky posited that the inability to love results in deep suffering. His literary works, infused with his personal struggles and time in prison, delve into this theme. The notion of hell as an internal fracture feels particularly relevant in today's world, where virtual interactions often lack true empathy and connection. Dostoevsky's wisdom underscores the vital importance of authentic human relationships.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: YouTuber Rusty Cage goes viral after testing how long a Lone Star tick can survive
item.description: YouTuber Rusty Cage gained popularity for his tick survival experiment. He filmed a Lone Star tick surviving underwater for five days. The tick also endured loud music and a 24-hour freezer test. This unusual research captured millions of views online. Cage's experiment has sparked widespread discussion across social media platforms.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Who is Connor Murphy’s alleged girlfriend? What we know about the late influencer’s relationship with the woman 10 years younger
item.description: Connor Murphy’s girlfriend has remained anonymous, but reports say she is 22 years old and had been in a relationship with the influencer for about three years. After his death in Thailand, she said she had never seen him use drugs and could not explain his reported behaviour before the incident. Authorities are still investigating the case.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Scientists invented a fake eye disease to see if AI chatbots could spot it, but the experiment took an unexpected turn
item.description: In a groundbreaking experiment, researchers created a fictitious eye disease, bixonimania, to evaluate the accuracy of AI chatbots. Many large language models accepted this non-existent condition as real, emphasizing the dangers of misinformation. Additionally, scientists found themselves referencing bogus research papers without realizing their inauthenticity, showcasing how AI learns patterns from the internet rather than confirming facts.
item.dc:creator: TOI Tech Desk

item.title: One of the world's largest T. rex skeletons is heading to auction with a price tag of up to $30 million
item.description: A remarkable Tyrannosaurus rex fossil, affectionately dubbed Gus, is set to hit the auction block at Sotheby's. Measuring an impressive 38 feet in length, this nearly complete skeleton is anticipated to attract bids ranging from twenty to thirty million dollars. Its outstanding preservation and size underscore Gus's significance within the growing market for collectible dinosaur fossils.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: US launches major probe into H-1B visa 'fraud'; Cognizant under scanner
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Will hit even deeper': Trump warns Iran as Tehran vows 'no American soldier will return alive'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists say NASA should quarantine alien life on the Moon before any space samples are brought back to Earth
item.description: A new policy paper proposes a lunar biocontainment facility for extraterrestrial samples. This secure lunar laboratory would analyze samples before Earth transport. Such a system significantly reduces risks to Earth's biosphere. The Moon offers accessibility and isolation for this crucial quarantine. This preparation ensures scientific responsibility as space exploration advances.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Connor Murphy cause of death: New details emerge after influencer's tragic death in Thailand
item.description: Fitness influencer Connor Murphy, 32, died in Thailand as authorities investigate the circumstances surrounding his death. Police said witnesses reported erratic behaviour before the incident, while investigators later recovered unused syringes and unidentified pills during a search. Murphy's girlfriend denied seeing him use drugs. An autopsy and toxicology tests are underway to determine the official cause of death, as fans mourn the popular fitness creator.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Scientists in Singapore have found a way to transform shrimp shell waste into 'carbon-negative' hydrogen fuel, turning biomass waste into valuable climate solutions
item.description: Researchers have pioneered a groundbreaking method that transforms organic waste into hydrogen fuel, while simultaneously generating protein suitable for aquaculture feed and calcium carbonate. This innovative technology aims to achieve a carbon-neutral process, capturing more carbon dioxide than it emits. Ongoing investigations are focused on potential commercial uses for this adaptable technology, though scaling up remains a notable hurdle to achieving widespread use.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Trump's Board of Peace planning pilot 'humanitarian zone' in south Gaza: Official
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Towering trees more than 200 feet tall may not be as vulnerable to drought as scientists thought, new study reveals how Southeast Asia’s dipterocarps efficiently move water
item.description: Recent studies unveil that massive trees in Southeast Asia possess remarkable adaptations in their water transport mechanisms. These trees, known for reaching impressive heights, can effectively draw water upward, defying the challenges posed by gravity. This groundbreaking research, focusing on Malaysia's dipterocarps, shows that taller trees feature broader xylem vessels, offering fresh insights into their resilience amidst climate change.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Lust for murder': German doctor who killed 15 patients gets life term; suspected in 76 more deaths
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: AI helped scientists discover two new superconductors, bringing them closer to a room-temperature breakthrough that could change electronics
item.description: Artificial intelligence is now speeding up the discovery of new superconductors. Scientists have identified two new superconducting materials using this advanced approach. This breakthrough brings researchers closer to achieving room-temperature superconductivity. Such a discovery could revolutionize energy transmission and electronics significantly. The international SuperC consortium aims for this goal within the next decade.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Telegram 'drug rape' network that shocked Germany! How secret codes were used to assault women
item.description: German prosecutors have discovered disturbing Telegram groups where members discussed plans for sexual assaults and drugging. They cleverly disguised their actions using terms like 'cars' and 'fuel.' Years of posts revealed numerous attacks on incapacitated individuals, with several members already convicted. Ongoing investigations aim to uncover the full extent of this network, raising significant concerns about the misuse of online platforms and their global repercussions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Mysterious 'space balls' twice the size of basketballs wash ashore on Australian beach, scientists say the discovery is more common than many people think
item.description: Metallic spheres have mysteriously appeared on an Australian beach, sparking local alarm. After thorough investigation, authorities determined the oddities were actually remnants of space debris—specifically pressure vessels from rocket launch vehicles that survived their fiery return to Earth. With the surge in space explorations, experts predict that encounters with such debris will become more frequent as launches increase.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Red scare by the red: Trump and Republicans test anti-communist message for midterm elections
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
